regime_reason: Tuesday-Wednesday's risk-off dip on US-Iran escalation reversed into a broad Thursday rally (Nasdaq +1.2%, S&P +0.8%, Dow +0.4%) as oil fell and semis led, but VIX is still elevated near 17 (vs ~16.3 a week ago) and June CPI due 7/14 remains hot at 4.2% YoY, so this reads as a bounce inside a still-jumpy tape rather than a clean risk-on signal.

watchlist.note: Transocean: $1B+ Equinor rig contract (7/8); Valaris all-stock merger now faces a DOJ second-request delay and shareholder-fairness scrutiny, earnings 8/5.
Absci: Truist/H.C. Wainwright/Guggenheim PT hikes to $15-17 (6/25-7/2) after positive Phase 1 ABS-201 data; +47% week, +87% month.
Hudson Technologies: 10% owner Hartree Partners extended its buying spree through 7/7 (~$4.5M total since 6/30), sustained insider conviction.

notes: User watchlist ticker DVLT (Datavault AI) stays avoid -- still ~$0.36 on a meme-coin launch and a speculative $700M materials-tokenization MOU, no durable fundamental catalyst, prior dilution history unresolved. Held positions: AVO's insider buying continues (10%-owner GlobalHarvest bought another $8.3M on 7/6, no negative news) while LRMR saw a Monday 17% selloff on an anaphylaxis safety signal in its nomlabofusp trial before rebounding on a 7/8 director buy -- worth watching, not yet a sell signal. VMAR stays on avoid (distressed financials, mixed insider signals, sold its Fort Lauderdale property 7/9 to raise cash).
